nodes.title: Informative References
nodes.summary: This subsection enumerates informative external standards and guidance that support the document’s safety management requirements and provide additional context for implementation.

nodes.title: General guidance by UK Health and Safety Executive
nodes.summary: This introductory guidance section anchors the document’s safety expectations in UK regulatory guidance and MOD policy publications to align defence safety practice with national H&S principles.

nodes.title: Safety Management System
nodes.summary: This section references external MOD and aviation safety governance documents to define how a Safety Management System should be planned, evidenced, and aligned with authoritative processes.

nodes.title: Legislation, Regulations, Standards, Policy and Approved Codes of Practice
nodes.summary: This section cites external codes of practice and MOD safety management frameworks to ensure legal and policy compliance is identified, documented, and agreed within the contract.

nodes.title: Contractors shall ensure that competent personnel are appointed
nodes.summary: This competency requirement references external competence frameworks and MOD sources to define expectations for safety practitioner capability and evidence of competence.

nodes.title: The contractor shall ensure the CSA is independent
nodes.summary: This subsection references an external IET code of practice to justify and standardize independence expectations for contractor safety auditors and independent assessment roles.

nodes.title: Safety Requirements, Hazard and Risk Analysis
nodes.summary: This section references external hazard analysis methods and related standards to support systematic identification, analysis, and management of hazards, risks, and safety requirements.

nodes.title: Cyber Security Considerations
nodes.summary: This subsection references cyber security standards to ensure hazard analysis explicitly considers cyber-related threats and controls as part of safety risk management.

nodes.title: Risk Management
nodes.summary: This section references MOD publications and safety standards to frame how contractors support MOD risk-to-life management and apply recognized risk management principles.

nodes.title: Defence Standard 00-055
nodes.summary: Defence Standard 00-055 defines requirements for safety of programmable elements in defence systems and is cited to align software/programmable safety expectations with this document’s safety management and hazard analysis requirements.

nodes.title: IEC 61508 Functional Safety of Electrical/Electronic/Programmable Electronic Safety-Related Systems
nodes.summary: IEC 61508 is the foundational functional safety standard for E/E/PE safety-related systems and is referenced to support recognized good practice for safety lifecycle and integrity in defence safety management.

nodes.title: Def Stan 00-251 Human Factors Integration for Defence Systems
nodes.summary: Def Stan 00-251 provides human factors integration guidance for defence systems and is cited to ensure hazard analysis and safety requirements consider human factors as a contributory cause and mitigation route.

nodes.title: Def Stan 05-138 Cyber Security for Defence Suppliers
nodes.summary: Def Stan 05-138 sets cyber security expectations for defence suppliers and is referenced to integrate cyber security considerations into hazard analysis and risk evaluation activities.

nodes.title: Def Stan 05-135 Avoidance of Counterfeit Materiel
nodes.summary: Def Stan 05-135 addresses avoidance of counterfeit materiel and is cited to ensure safety hazard analysis and risk considerations include supply-chain and counterfeit-related safety impacts.

nodes.title: STPA Handbook
nodes.summary: The STPA Handbook describes System-Theoretic Process Analysis and is referenced to justify and guide systematic hazard identification and analysis methods used within the document’s safety requirements process.

nodes.title: Regulatory Article (RA) RA1200
nodes.summary: RA1200 is an aviation regulatory article used to align safety management planning and assurance activities with recognized regulatory expectations referenced by the Safety Management System section.

nodes.title: POSMS
nodes.summary: POSMS (Project Oriented Safety Management System) provides MOD safety management process guidance and is referenced to structure safety planning, compliance management, and project safety governance within the document.

nodes.title: ASEMS DE&S Acquisition Safety and Environmental Management System
nodes.summary: ASEMS is the DE&S acquisition safety and environmental management system and is referenced to align safety management system expectations and supporting guidance with MOD acquisition processes.

nodes.title: UK Health and Safety Executive (HSE)
nodes.summary: The UK Health and Safety Executive is the national regulator and guidance authority for workplace safety and is referenced to ground the document’s safety approach in UK H&S expectations.

nodes.title: Joint Service Publication (JSP) 815
nodes.summary: JSP 815 is an MOD Joint Service Publication referenced to connect the document’s safety guidance with MOD policy and governance for health and safety management.

nodes.title: HSE Approved Codes of Practice
nodes.summary: HSE Approved Codes of Practice provide practical guidance for meeting legal duties and are referenced to ensure compliance expectations in the document are tied to recognized UK regulatory practice.

nodes.title: Defence Codes of Practice
nodes.summary: Defence Codes of Practice are referenced to ensure defence-specific compliance and good practice are considered alongside civil regulation when defining contractual safety obligations.

nodes.title: Managing Competence for Safety-Related Practitioners (MCSRP)
nodes.summary: MCSRP provides a competence management framework for safety-related practitioners and is referenced to support the document’s requirements for appointing and evidencing competent safety personnel.

nodes.title: IET Code of Practice for Competence for Safety-Related Systems Practitioners (COPCSRSP)
nodes.summary: COPCSRSP is an IET code of practice defining competence expectations for safety-related systems practitioners and is referenced to standardize competence criteria used in contractor staffing and assurance.

nodes.title: IET Code of Practice for Independent Safety Assessors (COPISA)
nodes.summary: COPISA defines good practice for independent safety assessors and is referenced to justify independence requirements for contractor safety auditing and independent safety assurance activities.

nodes.title: Knowledge in Defence website
nodes.summary: Knowledge in Defence (KID) is an MOD knowledge portal referenced as a source for safety-related competence and supporting information used across guidance and competency requirements.

nodes.title: JSP 822
nodes.summary: JSP 822 is an MOD Joint Service Publication referenced to support risk management and risk-to-life governance expectations within service provision and operational safety management.
